name: Santa María de Ordás and Las Omañas Loop
name_original: Santa María de Ordás e Irián — circular por Astorga
description: This difficult 18.5-mile (29.8 km) touring cycling route near Astorga offers tranquil scenery and passes through Irián Village.
region_intro: Touring cycling around Santa María De Ordás is characterized by its tranquil rural scenery and rolling hills within the broader Castile and León region. The landscape features varied terrain with notable elevation gains, offering routes that traverse peaceful countryside. This area provides a setting conducive to cycling through natural environments, with routes often passing through valleys and open spaces. The region's geography supports a network of routes suitable for exploring its natural beauty. Best touring cycling routes around Santa María De Ordás The most popular touring cycling route is Ordás and Tapia Loop from Sel, a 21.4 miles (34.4 km) trail that takes 2 hours 19 minutes to complete, offering a moderate ride through the local countryside. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Santa María de Ordás and Las Omañas Loop, a difficult 18.5 miles (29.8 km) path that includes significant elevation changes and scenic views. Local touring cyclists also love the La Garandilla Village loop from Santa María de Ordás, a 17.9 miles (28.8 km) trail leading through rural villages and open landscapes, often completed in about 1 hour 55 minutes. Touring cycling around Santa María De Ordás is defined by rolling hills, rural landscapes, and tranquil scenery. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to more challenging routes with greater elevation. The routes in Santa María De Ordás are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 1 review. More than 24 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Santa María De Ordás's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes around Santa María de Ordás?
A: Touring cycling around Santa María de Ordás is characterized by tranquil rural scenery and rolling hills within the broader Castile and León region. You'll encounter varied terrain with notable elevation gains, often passing through peaceful countryside, valleys, and open spaces. The landscape offers a pleasant cycling experience with natural views.

Q: Are there routes suitable for beginners or more experienced touring cyclists?
A: While there are no routes specifically classified as 'easy' for touring cycling, Santa María de Ordás offers options for both moderate and difficult levels. There are 3 moderate routes and 6 difficult routes available. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1037556746/ordas-and-tapia-loop-from-sel" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ordás and Tapia Loop from Sel&lt;/a&gt; provide a moderate challenge, while others like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1363111172/santa-maria-de-ordas-and-las-omanas-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa María de Ordás and Las Omañas Loop&lt;/a&gt; are more demanding with significant elevation changes.

Q: How challenging are the touring cycling routes in terms of elevation?
A: The routes around Santa María de Ordás feature notable elevation gains, contributing to their moderate to difficult classifications. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1363111172/santa-maria-de-ordas-and-las-omanas-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa María de Ordás and Las Omañas Loop&lt;/a&gt; includes approximately 470 meters of ascent, while the longer &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40323289" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tower of Tapia de la Ribera loop from Rioseco de Tapia&lt;/a&gt; involves around 845 meters of climbing.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while cycling in the area?
A: The area around Santa María de Ordás is rich in natural beauty. You can explore highlights such as the dramatic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4073598" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Calderones Gorge&lt;/a&gt; and the serene &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5558242" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of the Virgin of Manadero in the Cave of the Doves&lt;/a&gt;. For those interested in historical structures, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7187739" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camposagrado Bell Tower&lt;/a&gt; is also a point of interest.

Q: Are there any waterfalls along the touring cycling routes?
A: Yes, you can find natural attractions like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5558248" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Waterfall of the Arroyo de los Calderones&lt;/a&gt; in the vicinity of the touring cycling routes, offering a beautiful spot to visit.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes available?
A: Yes, many of the touring cycling routes in Santa María de Ordás are designed as loops. Popular circular options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31883343" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Garandilla Village loop from Santa María de Ordás&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through rural villages, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14989584" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riello – La Garandilla Village loop from Santa Lucia&lt;/a&gt;, offering another scenic circular journey.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Santa María de Ordás?
A: Given its location in Castile and León, the spring and autumn months generally offer the most pleasant conditions for touring cycling, with milder temperatures and beautiful scenery. Summer can be warm, while winter may bring colder weather, especially at higher elevations.

Q: Is Santa María de Ordás suitable for family touring cycling?
A: The routes around Santa María de Ordás are generally classified as moderate to difficult due to rolling hills and elevation gains. While there are no specifically 'easy' routes, families with older children or those accustomed to cycling on varied terrain might enjoy the moderate options, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1037556746/ordas-and-tapia-loop-from-sel" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ordás and Tapia Loop from Sel&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Santa María de Ordás?
A: The touring cycling routes in Santa María de Ordás are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 1 review. Cyclists often praise the tranquil rural scenery and the opportunity to explore the natural beauty of the Castile and León region.

Q: Are there options for longer touring cycling trips?
A: Yes, for those seeking longer rides, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40323289" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tower of Tapia de la Ribera loop from Rioseco de Tapia&lt;/a&gt; cover over 50 kilometers, providing a more extensive touring experience through the region's diverse landscapes.

Q: What are the parking options for touring cyclists in Santa María de Ordás?
A: As Santa María de Ordás is a rural area, parking is generally available in or near the villages that serve as starting points for the routes. It's advisable to look for designated parking areas or suitable roadside spots, respecting local regulations.

Q: Can I reach the touring cycling routes by public transport?
A: Public transport options to Santa María de Ordás and its immediate surroundings may be limited due to its rural nature. It's recommended to check local bus schedules or consider driving to a starting point, especially if you're bringing your own touring bicycle.
tips:
Curious circular tower with a fireplace and everything! It is quite well preserved and until recently it was possible to access its interior, which is also amazing.

You can read more about the tower and the legends that surround it in this interesting article: https://www.eldiario.es/castilla-y-leon/provincias/leon/ruta-tierra-torreon-santa-maria-ordas-leyenda -don-ares-omana_1_6114155.html

With only about twenty inhabitants, Irián is a quiet place that few pass by anymore, but it is the typical place that is worth visiting with a bike, not so much for its architecture but to appreciate its peaceful heartbeat.

faqs.question: How long does it take to complete the Santa María de Ordás e Irián — circular por Astorga route?
faqs.answer: This touring bicycle route typically takes around 2 hours and 26 minutes to complete. However, this duration can vary based on your cycling pace and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route is largely composed of paved tracks, offering a relatively smooth surface for touring bicycles. Despite this, the overall difficulty is classified as 'difficult' due to its demanding nature and elevation changes.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: No, this route is classified as 'difficult' and requires very good physical fitness. It is not recommended for beginners or families with young children. More experienced touring cyclists will find it a rewarding challenge.

faqs.question: What points of interest can I see along the way?
faqs.answer: The route offers picturesque and tranquil scenery. You'll pass by the Tower of Santa María de Ordás and cycle through the peaceful Irián Village, a small settlement known for its serene atmosphere.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Santa María de Ordás e Irián — circular por Astorga route?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas for this exact trailhead are not detailed, Astorga serves as a good base. You can typically find parking within or on the outskirts of Astorga, and then cycle to the starting point of the circular route.

faqs.question: Is the Santa María de Ordás e Irián — circular por Astorga route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: As a touring bicycle route, dogs are generally not recommended to accompany cyclists directly on the trail, especially given its 'difficult' classification. If you plan to bring a dog, ensure they are safely secured in a bicycle trailer or carrier, and always keep them on a leash when off the bike, respecting local regulations.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle the Santa María de Ordás e Irián — circular por Astorga route. It is a public route accessible to all.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle this route is typically during spring or autumn when temperatures are milder. Summer can be very hot, and winter might bring colder weather or adverse conditions, making the 'difficult' route even more challenging.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes, pubs, or accommodation options directly along the route?
faqs.answer: The route passes through Irián Village, which is a small settlement. While it offers a peaceful atmosphere, amenities like cafes, pubs, or dedicated accommodation might be limited directly on the trail. It's advisable to carry sufficient water and snacks, and plan for services in Astorga before or after your ride.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with any other well-known cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, approximately 11% of this route also passes through sections of the Santovenia trail.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a touring bicycle ride on this route?
faqs.answer: Given the 'difficult' classification and 29.8 km distance, you should bring plenty of water, energy snacks, a repair kit for your bicycle, a helmet, appropriate cycling attire, and sun protection. A fully charged phone and a map or GPS device are also highly recommended.
